title: Beside vs Besides
shortSummary: Beside means next to someone or something. Besides means in addition to something.
quickAnswer: Use beside for position: next to. Use besides when you add another point, meaning in addition or also.
coreDifference: Beside is about location. Besides adds extra information.
englishExplanation: Beside usually means next to a person or thing. For example, your phone can be beside your laptop. Besides is used when you add another idea or item. For example, besides English, someone may also speak Spanish.

examples.sentence: The cat is sleeping beside the fireplace.
Besides math, he also teaches science.
I don't like that restaurant. Besides, it's too expensive.
The school is beside the park.
Besides English, she speaks French and German.

quiz.question: Come and sit ___ me.
quiz.explanation: Beside means next to a person or thing.

quiz.question: ___ his job, he studies at night.
quiz.explanation: Besides means in addition to another thing.

seo.title: Beside vs Besides: Difference, Examples, and Quiz | ClearWord Lab
seo.description: Learn the difference between beside and besides with simple explanations, natural examples, and practice questions.
